inMicrovmWritebackProof.issue: #8477 (MM-C5) branch/PR writeback under the user's own GitHub authorization — GREEN
inMicrovmWritebackProof.green: A real Firecracker microVM (admin trigger POST /api/admin/khala/cloud/runtime-dispatch -> mint owner-linked execution token -> bridge -> daemon -> microVM) booted from this image, checked out AgentFlampy/openagents@main, staged a 1-file diff, brokered AgentFlampy's OWN github-identity credential from the live SCM auth broker (owner-scoped), PUSHED a real scoped branch (never force, never base), opened a real PR, then POSTed the outcome to the live /api/khala/cloud/runtime-turn-writeback route. REAL ARTIFACTS on github.com: branch pylon/agent-computer-turn.wb-final-20260708000734 + PR https://github.com/AgentFlampy/openagents/pull/3. The route recorded the thread-scoped writeback.recorded event in authoritative khala_sync_staging (event_id event.private.agent_computer.writeback.e913a752-ee6a-4fa7-a6b5-521b7741cfb0, status=pull_request_opened, pullRequestUrl=.../pull/3, sequence 6, turn turn.wb-final-20260708000734). Exactly ONE exact owner-attributed token_usage_events row for the turn. microVM reclaimed (0 live firecracker, scratch wiped). The brokered credential never appeared in any event/result/log.
serverSeamFixesThatMadeItGreen: gate: resolveKhalaWritebackAuthorization accepts a usable brokerable github-IDENTITY authorization, not only a github_write_connections row (the push already proves authorization) — dd8ba4f7fd
broker: the SCM auth broker vends the credential OWNER's github-identity (openauth_user_id), not the shared execution-agent user_id — e2673c68e1
recorder: owner-scoped client group so different owners never collide on one Khala Sync client group — 5f710e7492
recorder: derive the event sequence from GREATEST(event_count, MAX(sequence)+1) so out-of-band events don't cause a runtime_event_exists collision — fc01cb8497

inMicrovmTurnProof.boot: microVM booted from the baked agent-computer rootfs; vsock guest agent (agent-guest.service) ready in ~3s
inMicrovmTurnProof.inGuestTurn: turn-runner ran INSIDE the microVM: real depth-1 checkout of octocat/Hello-World@7fd1a60b01f91b314f59955a4e4d4e80d8edf11d (in-guest git fetch egress now works) + real 1-file staged diff (AGENT_COMPUTER_TURN.md, 8 insertions), Khala-shaped runtime events streamed over vsock (turn.started/tool.call/tool.result/text.completed/turn.finished)
inMicrovmTurnProof.artifactCopyOut: result.json extracted microVM -> host; baseCommit matched the pinned commit
inMicrovmTurnProof.reclaim: microVM SIGKILLed, per-run scratch rootfs wiped, tap deleted
inMicrovmTurnProof.egressFix: baked image had systemd-networkd enabled with empty /etc/systemd/network/, which claimed eth0 and cleared the kernel ip=; fix = disable+mask systemd-networkd.service/.socket/-wait-online + mask systemd-resolved + static /etc/resolv.conf (8.8.8.8/1.1.1.1), then e2fsck -fy
inMicrovmTurnProof.modelTokenReceipt: PROVEN 2026-07-07 (re-baked phase-2 turn-runner): a real microVM turn from the baked image (rootfsDigest sha256:fe7d6606...) ran the in-guest turn-runner with a work-context `inference` block (AC1 staging agent bearer linked to the mobile ownerUserId, gateway baseUrl=openagents-monolith-staging, model=openagents/khala, no-meter secret). It made ONE POST /v1/chat/completions (no-meter header => gateway billing.mode=no_debit, ZERO gateway rows) then POST /api/khala/cloud/runtime-turn-usage => insertedTokenUsage:true. Authoritative khala_sync_staging: EXACTLY ONE exact token_usage_events row for the turn (id event.inference.served-tokens.khala-cloud-runtime.276237b445903380835eb1b1cd53d4e4; openagents/khala; in=906 out=198 reasoning=0 total=1104; usage_truth=exact; demand_kind=external; demand_source=khala_mobile_org_cloud_runtime; actor_user_id=the mobile owner) and ZERO non-runtime-usage gateway rows for the turn/thread. The exact-one-receipt single-charge money path is proven from inside a real Firecracker microVM.

baselineRootfs.source: firecracker-ci/v1.10/x86_64/ubuntu-22.04.ext4 (STOCK CI rootfs, NOT the baked agent-computer image)

provenLifecycle.boot: microVM booted in ~1s with its own guest kernel 5.10.223 (host runs 6.17), 2 vCPUs / 1024 MiB
provenLifecycle.execInGuest: real commands ran inside the microVM over ssh-on-tap (uname/nproc)
provenLifecycle.egress: HTTPS 200 from inside the microVM through host NAT (fetched 3662 real bytes from raw.githubusercontent.com; stock rootfs curl needs a CA bundle, TLS handshake reached without it)
provenLifecycle.reclaim: microVM destroyed + per-run scratch rootfs wiped, tap deleted

substrateProof.note: This proves the Firecracker-on-our-GCE substrate end-to-end. It is NOT a real mobile Khala turn: the baked agent-computer rootfs (git+bun+pylon #8473 executor), the control-plane guest transport, and the /v1/placement -> firecracker -> executor integration remain to be built before the #8503 DoD turn can run.

guestImage.rootfsSource: source-controlled debootstrap jammy seven-harness v24 bake at commit 9e6c638a2e91b8882311b82934e55b2646b57c08
guestImage.rootfsStatus: SEVEN-HARNESS V24 IMAGE LIVE BOOT GREEN 2026-07-23: provisionerKind=live, all seven pinned version probes returned code 0, artifacts were extracted, cleanupReceipt.tornDown=true, and the host retained zero Firecracker processes and zero runtime directories after the boot smoke.
guestImage.rootfsBuildScript: apps/pylon/deploy/agent-computer/build-agent-computer-rootfs.sh (source-controlled bake: debootstrap jammy + seven pinned harnesses + vsock guest agent + turn-runner + oa-workroomd + egress fix; seals sha256 + emits a refs-only bake receipt; run as root on the nested-virt bake host, then boot-smoke and re-pin rootfsDigest)

codex.note: The CX-3 in-VM codex_app_server lane (turn-runner `codexTurn` block: codex exec --json under the broker-redeemed scratch CODEX_HOME, exact usage receipt with tokenChargeMetered:false) is wired and fixture-proven in-repo; its live rung needs this binary baked + one real in-VM turn.

harnesses.credentialPolicy: Provider material is injected for one turn at runtime. No provider key, OAuth token, or subscription home is present in the image or bake receipt.
harnesses.credentialScanner: The turn-runner scans the staged diff before writeback and emits typed finding refs only. Host closeout still requires its isolation scanner and cleanup receipt.

portableSessionControl.contract: fixed PORT-03 stage/materialize/activate/continue/quiesce/checkpoint/export/abort/reclaim/wipeCapability protocol; checkpoint and capability bytes plus private continuation tasks cross only authenticated bounded seams; no arbitrary command surface

helperProfile.watcher: ready only after the pinned Node 24 recursive fs.watch process writes its generation-bound ready handshake; activation replay checks process liveness; quiesce and reclaim terminate it
helperProfile.pty: unsupported: no admitted PTY helper is present in this image
helperProfile.lsp: ready only after the npm-signature-verified TypeScript language server returns an LSP initialize response and reports the pinned TypeScript version; activation replay checks process liveness; quiesce and reclaim terminate it
helperProfile.dap: unsupported: debugpy 1.8.21 has an exact PyPI hash but no registry signature or PEP 740 provenance for the selected Linux x64 wheel, so no debug-adapter profile or binary is admitted into this image
helperProfile.native: unsupported: the TypeScript controller is not a native helper

typescriptLsp.tarballSha256Role: Registry provenance only. The installed bytes use package-lock sha512 integrity and npm signature verification.
typescriptLsp.signatureVerification: The bake runs npm audit signatures and requires invalid=[] and missing=[] before it admits the installed package tree.

typescript.tarballSha256Role: Registry provenance only. The installed bytes use package-lock sha512 integrity and npm signature verification.

typescriptLsp.liveness: generation-bound wrapper PID, child-exit coupling, and ready-file handshake
typescriptLsp.cleanup: LSP shutdown, wrapper-enforced child termination, and ready/state file removal

debugpyDapCandidate.blocker: PyPI returns no PEP 740 provenance for the selected wheel and publishes no registry signature. A hash does not satisfy the signed-artifact admission rule.

portableSessionControl.note: The recorded digest is the controller in the live v22 image. The image supervises a generation-bound recursive Node watcher and an npm-signature-verified TypeScript LSP. PTY, DAP, and native helpers remain explicit unsupported omissions. The LSP is typescript-language-server, not tsserver. debugpy remains uninstalled because its exact PyPI wheel has no signature or PEP 740 provenance.

modelTurnRuntime.note: Phase-2 model turn: when the work-context carries an `inference` block (agent bearer + gateway baseUrl + model + ownerUserId + no-meter secret), the turn-runner makes ONE POST /v1/chat/completions (mirroring runWithHostedKhalaGateway, carrying x-openagents-org-cloud-runtime-no-meter) then posts the EXACT model-token receipt to POST /api/khala/cloud/runtime-turn-usage (mirroring recordRuntimeTurnUsageReceipt + KhalaCloudRuntimeUsageIngestBody). The agent bearer is never serialized into events/result. The baked /opt/agent/turn-runner IS this build as of rootfsDigest sha256:fe7d6606... (re-compiled bun-linux-x64 from origin/main and re-baked 2026-07-07).
modelTurnRuntime.liveProof: PROVEN 2026-07-07 against openagents-monolith-staging. See inMicrovmTurnProof.modelTokenReceipt for the authoritative khala_sync_staging row (COUNT=1 exact, owner-attributed, 0 gateway rows). Remaining for the full #8503 dispatch DoD: (1) Seam A — a cloud-gcp intent->/v1/placement dispatcher that threads the `inference` block; (2) cloud oa-codex-control placement->cloud_vm.rs microVM branch + openagents.resource_usage_receipt.v1 lifecycle receipts (provision/active/idle/reclaim vm_seconds); (3) run the control daemon as a root-armed service so /v1/placement can boot Firecracker; (4) #8477 git writeback (branch/PR) from the in-guest turn. The in-VM turn here was driven through the same cloud_vm.rs run_cloud_vm_session path the placement branch will call, with an inference-bearing work-context.

workroomd.note: Agent Computer guest images must include the in-repo oa-workroomd release binary (not a private cloud/ checkout). Bake via build-workroomd-for-image.sh [--docker], then copy into the rootfs at /usr/local/bin/oa-workroomd before sealing digests.
